sections.paragraph:
δοξολογία, ας, ἡ (on the prefix δοξο-s. DELG s.v. δοκάω etc. II p. 291; Orig., De Or. 14, 2 p. 331, 6 al. in patristic lit.; Iambl., Myst 2, 10; TestAbr A 20 p.103, 29 [Stone p. 54]) paean, prayer ἐν τῇ ὥρα τῆς δ. αὐτου at the time of his prayer GJs 13:1.

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Iambl Iambl , phil., III–IV a.d.—List 5

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 258
